articles.title: The State of JavaScript 2025 Survey
articles.description: Each year, Devographics runs an epic survey of as many JavaScript community members as it can and turns the results into an interesting report on the state of the ecosystem – here’s the results from 2024. If you have the time, fill it in, especially as they format it in a way where you can actually learn about stuff as you go.
articles.author: Devographics

articles.title: React 19.2 Released
articles.description: The third release in a year for React, this time introducing new features like <Activity /> (a way to hide and restore the UI and internal state of its children), useEffectEvent, and improvements to Chrome DevTools' performance profiles so you can see more about React’s scheduling and the tree of components it’s working with. Oh, and how about partial pre-rendering?
articles.author: The React Team

articles.title: How Deno Protects Against npm Exploits
articles.description: The maintainers of the Deno runtime reflect on the recent npm ecosystem security issues and show off how Deno’s non-permissive explicit ‘secure by default’ model can help.
articles.author: Andy Jiang

articles.title: Cleaning House in Nx Monorepo: How I Removed 120 Unused Deps Safely
articles.description: Another win (mostly) for Knip, a tool that helps you declutter projects by finding unused dependencies.
articles.author: John James

articles.title: You Can Now Make PS2 Games in JavaScript
articles.description: Last week we mentioned running JavaScript in MS-DOS but you can take it to Sony’s Playstation 2 as well using a system built upon QuickJS.
articles.author: JSLegendDev

articles.title: How to Detect Safari and iOS Versions from JavaScript
articles.description: Naturally, progressive enhancement is the best policy, but if you need to..
articles.author: Evgeniy Valyaev

articles.title: Mastering npx: A Cheatsheet for npm and Node Power Users
articles.description: You’ve almost certainly used the npx command to easily run a command from an npm package (either local or remote) – it’s easy to use, but npx has a couple of other features and options to keep in mind.
articles.author: Liran Tal

articles.title: qjs: Run JavaScript in Go using QuickJS and Wazero
articles.description: A new Cgo-free JavaScript runtime option for integrating JavaScript into apps written in Go. It uses a fork of QuickJS that’s compiled to WebAssembly and then run using Wazero.
articles.author: Nguyen Ngoc Phuong and Contributors

articles.title: SpaceTime 7.11: A Lightweight Timezone Library
articles.description: Use this to calculate times in other timezones. Has a Moment-like API but is immutable. No dependencies. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Spencer Kelly

articles.title: Jeasx: A Lightweight SSR Framework That Uses JSX
articles.description: A server side rendering framework built on top of JSX and Fastify. It doesn’t use React, but if you want to keep using JSX while keeping your server side lightweight, it’s an option. The JSX functionality is provided by the author’s jsx-async-runtime package. v1.9 was just released.
articles.author: Maik Jablonski

articles.title: 📊 Vue ECharts 8.0: Vue.js Component for Apache ECharts
articles.description: Apache ECharts is a popular, powerful JavaScript charting library we’ve linked many times and this project makes it easier to use in Vue.js apps. v8.0 upgrades to ECharts 6.0. GitHub repo.
articles.author: GU Yiling and ECOMFE

articles.title: modern-tar: Zero-Dependency Streaming tar Parser and Writer
articles.description: As in the classic tar archive format.
articles.author: Ayuhito

articles.description: Monthly roundups aplenty from the Svelte project, team Astro, and Viteland!
It was recently discovered that Electron-based apps were causing system-wide lag on macOS 26/Tahoe due to changes in a private API. Electron 38.2, 37.6 and 36.9.2 fix the bug.
📺 The creators of popular documentaries ▶️ about Vue.js, ▶️ React, and ▶️ Node.js are about to release a new one about Vite. It lands next week; ▶️ here's a 1-minute teaser trailer.

The newly relaunched JSConf event returns to Maryland on October 14-16. Tickets are still available and they gave us a JSCONF25NEWSLETTER code which gives a discount. (Note: We have no financial relationship with the event.)
🤖 Google AI Studio has added the ability to generate Angular apps.
The release of Bun 1.3, expected yesterday, has been pushed back to next Monday.
Anime.js 4.2 – Powerful JavaScript animation engine.
npm-check-updates 19.0 – Find newer versions of packages dependencies than your package.json allows.
pnpm 10.18, Oxlint 1.19, Bun 1.2.23, Deno 2.5.3

articles.title: The History of Core Web Vitals
articles.description: This is a comprehensive chronicle of the story behind Core Web Vitals over the years, looking at how the initiative has evolved, and its broader impact on web performance. The suggestion here is that thanks to Core Web Vitals, when taken together, Chrome users have been saved from a combined total of 30,000 years of waiting!
articles.author: Addy Osmani

articles.title: What's New in View Transitions (2025 Update)
articles.description: Bramus brings us up to speed on the various enhancements and recently shipped features now found in the View Transitions API (an area of focus for Interop 2025). He also shares how we can go about leveraging these improvements for smoother web app animations, by way of code examples and demos.
articles.author: Bramus (Google)

articles.title: 🎨 A Pragmatic Guide to Modern CSS Colors
articles.description: The first in a two-part series looking over modern CSS color capabilities. If you’ve not approached using newer CSS color techniques yet this is an excellent, easy to read primer, filled with practical examples.
articles.author: Kevin Powell

articles.title: Europe’s Cookie Law 'Messed Up The Internet.' Brussels Wants to Fix It
articles.description: “the Commission is pondering how to tweak the rules to include more exceptions or make sure users can set their preferences on cookies once (for example, in their browser settings) instead of every time they visit a website”.
articles.author: Ellen O'Regan

articles.title: Stop Ignoring the Browser: The Biggest Frontend Shift in a Decade
articles.description: Makes the case for native browser APIs as viable alternatives to using frameworks like React & Angular, plus looks at how such a change might impact developer workflows.
articles.author: Alexander T. Williams

articles.title: ▶  What’s New in DevTools 139-141
articles.description: A quick recap looking at the latest DevTools features in Chrome, including changes to the network panel, Baseline information, and new AI assistance.
articles.author: Matthias Rohmer

articles.title: Getting Creative with shape-outside
articles.description: Andy shows us (by way of some handy demos) how CSS Shapes can open up our options on how to layout a page composition.
articles.author: Andy Clarke

articles.title: How to Detect Safari and iOS Versions from JavaScript
articles.description: Naturally, progressive enhancement is the best policy, but if you need to..
articles.author: Evgeniy Valyaev

articles.title: Accented: A Frontend Library for Continuous Accessibility Testing
articles.description: Just add a few lines to your app and the library (powered by Axe) will add interactive callouts next to elements with accessibility issues. There’s a write-up and you can try a live demo on Stackblitz.
articles.author: Pavel Pomerantsev

articles.title: Size Matters: A Fluid Type Scale Generator
articles.description: The process of generating the code (which uses the CSS clamp() function) is provided in a handy multi-step process that includes a live preview, and you can export in CSS, Sass, JSON, and more.
articles.author: Ash Bryant

articles.title: itty.dev: Small But Powerful Libraries for Modern Web Apps
articles.description: Includes a router, fetcher, packager, utility for console styling, and more. All libraries are small and intuitive to use.
articles.author: Kevin R. Whitley

articles.title: Harmonizer: Сreate Accessible, Consistent, Color Palettes for UIs
articles.description: Attempts to solve some of the problems inherent in design system colors by “generating color with perceptually consistent lightness and contrast”. This helps, for example, with text contrast. Available as a web tool or Figma plugin.
articles.author: evil martians

articles.title: StreamTest: A Chrome Extension for Monitoring WebRTC Connection Health
articles.description: It provides detailed data on video stream quality, including FPS, bitrate, resolution, connection status, packet loss, delays, etc.
articles.author: Fora Soft
